requirements.title: Implement process_data function
requirements.description: Create a Python function named process_data that accepts a list as input parameter and returns a sorted version of that list.
acceptance_criteria: Function must accept exactly one parameter which is a list
Function must return a new sorted list without modifying the original input list
Function must handle lists containing integers, floats, or strings
Function must use Python's built-in sorted() function or list.sort() method appropriately

requirements.title: Handle empty list input
requirements.description: The process_data function should properly handle empty list inputs by returning an empty list.
acceptance_criteria: When process_data([]) is called, it returns []
Empty list input does not raise any exceptions
Returned empty list is a new list object, not the same reference

requirements.title: Maintain input list immutability
requirements.description: The original input list should not be modified by the process_data function.
acceptance_criteria: Original list remains unchanged after calling process_data
Function creates and returns a new sorted list rather than sorting in-place
Input list reference equality check shows different objects for input and output

requirements.title: Support common data types
requirements.description: The function should handle lists containing common Python data types including integers, floats, and strings.

requirements.title: Include function documentation
requirements.description: The process_data function should include proper docstring documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Function includes a docstring with description of purpose
Docstring specifies parameter type and name
Docstring specifies return type and description
Docstring includes at least one usage example
